chief_complaint: Occult urosepsis in profoundly immunosuppressed patient with deceptively normal vital signs
clinical_reasoning: CRITICAL: This patient is profoundly immunosuppressed (methotrexate 15 mg weekly + prednisone 7.5 mg daily) with a 6-day bounce-back after UTI treatment. The vital signs appear reassuringly normal — T 36.4°C, HR 78, BP 118/68, RR 20, SpO2 96% — but EVERY SINGLE ONE IS DECEPTIVELY REASSURING. (1) TEMPERATURE: Prednisone suppresses the hypothalamic fever response. Immunosuppressed patients frequently present afebrile with fulminant sepsis. Absence of fever is NOT absence of infection. (2) HEART RATE: Metoprolol 50 mg BID is a potent beta-blocker. HR 78 in a septic patient on metoprolol means the true sympathetic response is being pharmacologically obliterated. Without the beta-blocker, this patient's heart rate could be 110-130. (3) BLOOD PRESSURE: 118/68 looks normal — but her baseline is 148/82 (documented across 3 clinic visits). This is a 30-point SYSTOLIC DROP representing relative hypotension and early distributive shock. (4) qSOFA = 0 and SIRS = 0 — but these screening tools are KNOWN TO FAIL in immunosuppressed and beta-blocked patients. The literature shows qSOFA sensitivity drops to <50% in patients on immunosuppressive therapy. This is textbook occult sepsis in an immunocompromised host. The E. coli from her UTI culture showed quinolone resistance — TMP-SMX may have failed, allowing bacteraemia to develop. STAT lactate and blood cultures NOW. Do not wait for the patient to become overtly septic — by then she will be in refractory shock with a mortality rate exceeding 40%.
vital_sign_concerns: T 36.4°C — afebrile is EXPECTED in immunosuppressed sepsis (prednisone blunts fever)
HR 78 — MASKED by metoprolol 50 mg BID (true HR likely 110-130 without beta-blockade)
BP 118/68 — RELATIVE HYPOTENSION: 30-point systolic drop from baseline 148/82
RR 20 — subtle tachypnea, may be early metabolic compensation for lactic acidosis
qSOFA 0/3 and SIRS 0/4 — screening tools UNRELIABLE in immunosuppressed + beta-blocked patients
risk_factors: PROFOUND immunosuppression: methotrexate + prednisone dual therapy
Relative hypotension (30-point systolic drop from documented baseline)
6-day bounce-back after UTI treatment — treatment failure
E. coli with quinolone resistance — possible ESBL producer
3 UTIs in 18 months — structural or immunological vulnerability
Chronic prednisone — adrenal insufficiency under physiologic stress
